'''Sadiq Adebiyi'''  {{Audio|LL-Q34311_(yor)-Mophefavour-Sadiq_Adebiyi.wav|Listen|help=no}}(born 8 January 1997) is a Nigerian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]] and [[Nigeria national rugby league team|Nigeria]] at international level.

Adebiyi played for [[London Broncos]] between 2015 and 2021 with loan spells at  [[Hemel Stags]], [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] and [[London Skolars]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]],<ref name="Academy captain, Sadiq Adebiyi, joins first team in 2017">{{cite web|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/adebiyi-joins-first-team-2017/|title=Academy captain, Sadiq Adebiyi, joins first team in 2017|publisher=London Broncos|date=21 May 2016|access-date=16 September 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160615030205/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/adebiyi-joins-first-team-2017/ |archive-date=15 June 2016 |url-status=dead}}</ref> and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Adebiyi was born in [[Lagos]], Nigeria.

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
Adebiyi made his debut for the Broncos in 2015 and was a member of the first team squad from 2017 until 2021.<ref name="Academy captain, Sadiq Adebiyi, joins first team in 2017"/> 
[[File:Sid Adebiyi.jpg|thumb|right|Adebiyi pre-game for the London Broncos in 2016]]
He spent time loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Hemel Stags]], [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] and the [[London Skolars]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

Adebiyi also spent time loan from [[London Broncos|London]] at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].
[[File:Sadiq Adebiyi London Broncos.jpg|thumb|right|Adebiyi warming up the Broncos at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
Injuries and the COVID-19 pandemic limited his opportunities in 2020 and 2021. In 2020 he suffered a [[Pectoral muscles|pectoral injury]] that ruled him out for several months.<ref name="Broncos suffer double blow">{{cite web |url=https://www.totalrl.com/broncos-suffer-double-blow/ |title=Broncos suffer double blow |publisher=Total RL |date=16 February 2020 |access-date=25 March 2021}}</ref> 

In 2021, Adebiyi ruptured his [[Achilles tendon]] and missed most of the Championship season.<ref>{{cite news |title=London Broncos forward makes Super League switch |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-forward-sadiq-adebiyi-makes-super-league-switch/ |access-date=7 October 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=15 October 2021}}</ref>

During the time at London he made 49 appearances scoring 11 tries.

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 15 October 2021 Adebiyi signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=15 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Sadiq Adebiyi: Wakefield Trinity sign London Broncos forward on one-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58917918}}</ref> During the 2022 season he made three appearances for Trinity.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Newcastle Thunder bring in trio on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-thunder-bring-in-trio-on-loan/ |access-date=7 October 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=27 April 2022}}</ref>

He spent time on loan at the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Newcastle Thunder bring in trio on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-thunder-bring-in-trio-on-loan/ |access-date=7 October 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=27 April 2022}}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars===
Adebiyi joined Keighley on a two-year deal in October 2022.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Pattison |first1=Cam |title=Wakefield Trinity forward latest Super League man to join Championship club |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/wakefield-trinity-forward-latest-super-league-man-to-join-championship-club-60154/ |access-date=7 October 2022 |work=Rugby League News |date=7 October 2022}}</ref>

===London Broncos (rejoin)===
On 27 October 2023 it was reported that he would re-join the London Broncos for the 2024 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=27 Oct 2023|title=Sadiq Adebiyi: London Broncos re-sign forward on two-year contract|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67244034}}</ref>

==International==
Adebiyi represented [[Nigeria national rugby league team|Nigeria]] twice in 2019 captaining his national team as they won the [[2019 MEA Rugby League Championship|MEA Championship]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Nigeria beat Morocco, win maiden Middle East Africa Rugby League |url=https://www.pressreader.com/nigeria/the-guardian-nigeria/20191007/282230897446329 |access-date=7 October 2022 |work=The Guardian (Nigeria) |agency=pressreader.com |date=7 October 2019}}</ref>
